MINIMA

A tiny retro game experiment written in Zig and WebAssembly.

The current version is mostly a low-level runtime playground: fixed-size pixels, shared memory, keyboard input, tile maps, simple scenes, and tiny battle prototypes.

How to play

For now, the controls use a small virtual button layout.

Arrow keys
Move the menu cursor or battle cursor.
Space
Start / confirm in menus.
Y
A button.
X
B button.
A
X button.
S
Y button.
Q / W
L / R buttons.
Enter
Select button. In battle, this currently returns to the menu.

Project philosophy

MINIMA is a small attempt to understand games closer to the metal, without immediately hiding everything behind engines, frameworks, assets, tooling, or modern convenience.

The visual and technical direction is deliberately constrained: low resolution, 8×8 tiles, tiny sprites, simple colour choices, and a runtime that exposes its own memory layout to the browser.

The game direction is loosely inspired by old computer RPGs, tactical games, and board-like combat systems: Ultima, Might and Magic, chess, and Into the Breach are all somewhere in the background.

The C64-ish constraints are not pure nostalgia. They are a way to make resource use visible again: memory, pixels, state, input, timing, and representation all become things to think about.

For more info and source code, see GitHub.
